Mexico’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, is going through her most delicate moment just as she reaches a year and a half in office. It is the steepest fall so far in her term, although approval ratings remain high at 68%, according to an Enkoll poll conducted for EL PAÍS and W Radio. To the crisis triggered by the indictment of the governor of Sinaloa, along with nine other senior officials accused by a New York prosecutor of collaborating with drug traffickers, is added a worrying economic weakness that threatens the viability of social policies—a flagship of the leftist Morena government. The historic U.S. indictment against the Sinaloa governor, Rubén Rocha—the first ever against a sitting governor—also had a precursor that accelerated pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump, who counts Mexico as one of his favorite targets.","score":0.8364614,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.riotimesonline.com/sheinbaum-approval-record-low-april-2026-poll","title":"Corruption Fears Drive Sheinbaum Approval to All-Time Low","content":"Sheinbaum approval dropped to 51% in April, three points below March and at least 15 points below a year ago, per Bloomberg-AtlasIntel.","score":0.77589405,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/RegeneracionMx/posts/summary-sheinbaum-welcomes-83-approval-rating-it-represents-the-empowerment-of-t/1070688465095274","title":"SUMMARY Sheinbaum welcomes 83% approval rating","content":"According to the latest survey published by El Financiero, President Claudia Sheinbaum currently holds a 72% approval rating among citizens.","score":0.7040996,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://mexiconewsdaily.com/news/poll-president-sheinbaum-69","title":"EF poll: President Sheinbaum's approval rating at 69%","content":"President Sheinbaum concluded her first full year in office with a 69% approval rating in December, according to the most recent national poll conducted by the newspaper El Financiero. Though 69% is considered a healthy rating for a president, the numbers show a steady decline since February 2025, when Sheinbaum enjoyed an 85% overall approval rating in the same survey. The telephone survey — conducted by phoning 800 Mexican adults across the country Dec. 10-17 — revealed that while Sheinbaum’s government continues to receive negative ratings in security and corruption (also reflected in a late-November Enkoll poll), opinions are divided with regard to the government’s economic performance. With regard to the attributes the public perceives in the president, 58% rated her positively in honesty, 57% lauded her leadership, and 52% approved her ability to deliver results, the latter percentage an improvement compared to El Financiero’s November survey results, when only 47% responded positively.","score":0.6161283,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.81,"request_id":"172671b2-61ac-4b8e-8b69-33570731ace8"}
